utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: next patient is paul edwards , date of birth is january 15th 1962 . so he's a 59 year old hiv positive gentleman here for hypogonadism . patient was last seen on november 24th 2020 . his notable things are number one , he is on 1 milliliter every 10 days , uh , his levels were less than 300 to begin with . he's on finasteride currently . he also takes cialis daily so he takes all his pills just from me . um , patient's other area of concern is gynecomastia which is ... which we will discuss with him today . his last psa was 0.66 and his last testosterone was greater than 1,500 .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , how are you today ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: all right , how have you been ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'm good .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: good , good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: have you lost some weight or are you at least putting on some muscle ? you look trim .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , i think i'm pretty much the same as i've always been .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: really ? okay , maybe it's just your black shirt . makes you look thin .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , i guess that's it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so health wise , how is everything going ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: good , the testosterone's going well .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: that's great .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , it helped me out . i feel good , more vigorous , sleeping well and i think it's having some positive effects . not so much physically because like i said i've- i've been this way my whole life , but i'm seeing some good improvements in my bloodwork .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , well that's good .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: so the finasteride i'm only taking half a pill , it's the 5 milligram one .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah , i remember you telling me that .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: and cialis , on the days i work out i take 5 milligrams otherwise i take two and a half milligram pills , but , uh , i have been out of it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: but overall i'm doing well , i'm actually taking the correct steps to get my life together .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: good . it's always great to hear . well let's take a look . uhm , i'm gon na listen to your heart and lungs .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: please use my general exam template , all right . just take a few breaths .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: in and out .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right , everything sounds good , no concerns there .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: great . so i wanted to show you something .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: sure .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: look at this .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , this is your cholesterol ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , my cholesterol and triglycerides . uh , i used to have high triglycerides , you see they were 265 milligrams per deciliter , and i took my first dose of the testosterone on the 28th .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: right .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: now 5 months later look at my numbers .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: wow , that's remarkable .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: is it the test ? it's the only change .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i do n't know , i have n't honestly seen many guys over the years that have cholesterol problems and this . i mean there's a big correlation between diabetic control and testosterone replacement , meaning those who get good levels of their test see their diabetic control improve .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: but i have n't seen a lot of data on the impact on cholesterol . regardless , we will take it .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i agree . i was very impressed with my triglycerides and was just wondering if the test may be what's helping .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah , that's an unbelievable difference .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: 145 milligrams per deciliter from 265 milligrams per deciliter is awesome . i also read that it- it's cardioprotective .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: absolutely .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: my red blood cell count has increased .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah , i saw that . it's fine though .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: stable .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: your psa today is also , uh , is good also . it's , uh , .6 i think .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah . , is it ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah , it was .5 last year and anything under 4 is good .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: nice , that's good news .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so it just needs to be checked every year or so .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: so in terms of , uh , estrogen control i've been hearing that indole-3-carbinol , or broccoli extract , supposedly can improve my estrogen levels . have you ever heard of it ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah , i've heard of it but i have n't had anybody consistently use it . i mean , your levels are fine and we checked your estra- estradiol and it was not elevated , so .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i would argue that we could test that in the fall if you want , but we do n't need to do , uh , do any more tests ... any more than test once a year , excuse me .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , what about increasing my testosterone to 175 milligrams ? i'm at 140 now .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: well , your levels are high .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: are they right now ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: well , i mean they were last time .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah but i just- just injected though , or i had right before that was taken .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i know . i know you had then , uh , when did you inject this time ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i figure i'm on my eighth day today .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: so i'm due to dose on thursday or friday .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i have a little med calendar and i put checks and ts on it . that helps me .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: that's a great idea . so look , the biggest issue i've seen , even if your levels today are around 700 , is that your peaks are getting greater than 1,500 , putting you at a higher chance of needing to come off due to blood thickness . and your risk will only astronomically go up the higher the dose that we go on .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: you look well , your levels are good and you're feeling well .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , i'm feeling good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'm going to be blunt . unfortunately this happens often where you're feeling good but you want to feel really good . i mean , i get it and this is why people get into problems with this stuff , right ? it's like , back in the day when it was n't prescribed by doctors and people would get it at gyms and stuff and they would take huge doses . and then they would have a heart attack at 50 .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , they have to be taking a lot .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: likely they are taking more than testosterone , but still .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: and they are taking stuff for a long time .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: true . but right now i would not change your dose .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: make sense ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it does , i appreciate the discussion .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: no problem . what pharmacy are you using ? have you changed it or anything ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no changes , i use walmart pharmacy . i do need more cialis and finasteride .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i would prefer the paper prescription .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: for all of them ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sure .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right , will do . i'm gon na get your prescriptions .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , thank you .

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 59-year-old male with a history of HIV presenting for hypogonadism.
Hypogonadism
Gynecomastia
The doctor asks how the patient is doing health-wise.
The patient reports that testosterone therapy is going well.
The patient reports feeling vigorous, sleeping well, and seeing improvements in blood work.
The patient reports taking half of a 5 mg Finasteride tablet daily.
The patient reports taking 5 mg of Cialis on exercise days and 2.5 mg on non-exercise days.
The patient is actively trying to get his life together.
The doctor performs a physical examination of the heart and lungs.
The doctor notes that the heart and lung examination findings are normal.
The patient reports his triglycerides were previously 265 mg/dL.
The patient asks if the testosterone therapy is responsible for the changes in his blood work.
The patient asks if testosterone is helping his triglyceride levels.
The patient reports his current triglyceride level is 145 mg/dL.
The patient notes his red blood cell count has increased.
The doctor notes the patient's PSA level is 0.6 ng/mL.
The patient asks about the benefits of Indole-3-carbinol for estrogen control.
The doctor notes the patient's estradiol levels were previously normal.
The patient asks to increase his testosterone dose from 140 mg to 175 mg.
The patient confirms he had recently injected testosterone before his last blood work, which showed levels over 1500 ng/dL.
The patient uses a medication calendar to support compliance.
The doctor advises against increasing the testosterone dose due to the risk of high levels and blood thickness.
The doctor denies the request to change the testosterone dose.
The patient requests a refill for Cialis and Finasteride.
The doctor provides paper prescriptions for Cialis and Finasteride.
